title: potentilla basaltica
intro: Potentilla basaltica, also known as Tibetan cinquefoil, is a flowering plant species in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to the Himalayan region and the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Potentilla basaltica include its ability to alleviate fever, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections and digestive issues.
